metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or fold into AC5) that states the specific observable outcome for the `setup-project.sh` update and supplies a proving command (e.g. `grep -F 'Autosubmit' infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` or equivalent) so the deliverable is verifiable in-session.
findings.finding: The `setup-project.sh` update is named as a Scope deliverable ('PLUS a setup-project.sh commit-message/update touch so the cutover is reflected in the audit trail') but has no corresponding Acceptance Criterion and no proving command. An executing agent has no in-session check to confirm this deliverable is complete.
findings.location: Scope paragraph, last sentence; Acceptance Criteria section (no matching AC)
findings.suggested_fix: Add to AC5: 'AND `grep -F "Autosubmit" infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` exits 0, confirming the audit-trail touch is committed.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend AC#5) requiring that the `setup-project.sh` commit message is updated to mention the `Autosubmit` label + ACL addition, so the refs/meta/config audit trail reflects the cutover.
findings.finding: The plan's Scope section names a 'setup-project.sh commit-message/update touch so the cutover is reflected in the audit trail' as a deliverable, but the hardcoded commit message on line 148 of setup-project.sh ('rebar project.config: LLM-Review + Verified labels + submit requirements + submit-type pin + feature-branch ACLs + Contributors submit gate') has no corresponding AC that verifies it was updated to mention the Autosubmit label/ACL addition. An implementer can satisfy all ACs without touching the commit message, making the Scope deliverable unverifiable.
findings.location: Scope section ('PLUS a setup-project.sh commit-message/update touch'); infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh line 148
findings.suggested_fix: Extend AC#5 with: 'and the `setup-project.sh` commit message (line 148) is updated to include `+ Autosubmit label + vote ACL`' — or add a separate verify_command: `grep -q 'Autosubmit' infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` targeting the commit message string.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC that verifies `setup-project.sh` has been updated (or its commit message records the cutover), so the described audit-trail deliverable is measurably complete.
findings.finding: The Scope section names `setup-project.sh` as an in-scope deliverable ('PLUS a `setup-project.sh` commit-message/update touch so the cutover is reflected in the audit trail'), but none of the six ACs verify this artifact exists or was updated.
findings.location: Scope section — '…PLUS a `setup-project.sh` commit-message/update touch…'; no corresponding AC
findings.suggested_fix: Add a criterion such as: '`setup-project.sh` is amended (or re-committed with a message referencing this cutover) so that `git log --oneline infra/gerrit/setup-project.sh` shows a commit contemporaneous with the refs/meta/config push, completing the audit trail.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify that `GET /a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` is a supported endpoint on the live Gerrit 3.14.1 instance (or specify a confirmed fallback verification command such as `GET /a/projects/rebar/labels/?detailed=FULL` filtered for `Autosubmit`) and document the auth mechanism (HTTP basic with admin credentials) in AC#5.
findings.finding: AC#5 mandates verifying the cutover via `GET /a/projects/rebar/labels/Autosubmit` (a specific per-label REST endpoint), but this endpoint surface has no prior use in the repository and is unverified against the live Gerrit 3.14.1 deployment. The endpoint path `/projects/{project}/labels/{label-id}` was added to Gerrit's REST API in the 3.x series, but the plan provides no verification command, no prior evidence of use, and no fallback if the endpoint returns 404 or is unavailable.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace or augment with: `GET /a/projects/rebar/labels/?detailed=FULL` (the list-labels endpoint, well-established in Gerrit 3.x) filtered for `Autosubmit`, and specify auth: `curl -u admin:<token> https://<host>/a/projects/rebar/labels/?detailed=FULL`. If the per-label endpoint IS confirmed available, cite the Gerrit 3.14 REST docs URL and the curl command with auth.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to AC#4 and AC#5 an explicit curl command with auth mechanism (e.g. `curl -u admin:<token> https://<gerrit-host>/a/projects/rebar/access`) and confirm the /access endpoint is reachable on the live instance (or confirm HTTPS + HTTP-basic auth are the correct preconditions).
findings.finding: AC#4 and AC#5 require capturing and diffing `GET /a/projects/rebar/access`, but this REST endpoint has no prior demonstrated use in the repository (only `GET /a/projects/rebar/config` has been used live), and the plan specifies no auth mechanism, no curl command, and no HTTPS precondition for the REST calls.
findings.location: AC#4: 'save the current GET /a/projects/rebar/access response'; AC#5: 'a post-cutover GET /a/projects/rebar/access diffs cleanly'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a one-line capture command to AC#4: `curl -s --fail -u "$GERRIT_ADMIN_USER:$GERRIT_ADMIN_HTTP_TOKEN" "https://${GERRIT_HOST}/a/projects/rebar/access" > infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json`. Mirror the same command for the post-cutover snapshot in AC#5. This matches the HTTP-basic auth pattern already used by other Gerrit REST consumers in the project.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the prose diff assertion in AC5 with a concrete structured proving command that enforces the 'ONLY' constraint, e.g. a `jq`-based comparison that extracts new keys from the post-cutover response and asserts exactly two `label-Autosubmit` entries and nothing else.
findings.finding: AC5's post-cutover access diff assertion ('diffs cleanly against … showing ONLY the added label-Autosubmit vote grants (no other ACL drift)') is expressed in prose without a structured, anchored proving command. The 'ONLY' cardinality constraint cannot be mechanically verified — a visual diff could miss additional ACL entries if the JSON response order shifts or extra whitespace is introduced.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria, AC5: 'a post-cutover GET /a/projects/rebar/access diffs cleanly...'
findings.suggested_fix: Example (illustrative): `diff <(jq -S 'del(.local["refs/heads/*"]["label-Autosubmit"])' post.json) <(jq -S '.' pre.json)` exits 0 (meaning the ONLY difference is the label-Autosubmit entries), plus `jq -e '[.local["refs/heads/*"]["label-Autosubmit"] | length == 2]' post.json`.
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a validation gate to the snapshot step: require the operator to confirm the captured JSON contains an HTTP 200 response and a recognisable project ACL structure (e.g., `jq '.local' access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json | grep -q refs/heads`) before committing it alongside the label change.
findings.finding: The pre-cutover snapshot capture (GET /a/projects/rebar/access → access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json) is described as a manual operator step but has no error-handling or validation guidance: if the endpoint returns an error, a redirect, or unexpected JSON, the plan gives no instruction on whether to abort the subsequent refs/meta/config push.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'Pre-cutover access baseline captured BEFORE the label is pushed: save the current GET /a/projects/rebar/access response to infra/gerrit/access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json'
findings.suggested_fix: Prepend to the snapshot AC: 'Verify the response is HTTP 200 and `jq .inheritsFrom access-snapshot-pre-autolander.json` returns the expected parent project; abort and do not push if the check fails.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the credential/auth mechanism for the `GET /a/projects/rebar/access` snapshot REST call (e.g., HTTP Basic with the admin account, referencing the same `GERRIT_ADMIN_SSH_KEY`-owning identity used by `setup-project.sh`, or a dedicated read token) and confirm HTTPS is enforced for the call — so the access posture of this boundary-crossing read is not left implicit.
findings.finding: The AC for capturing the pre-cutover access snapshot (`GET /a/projects/rebar/access`) does not declare the access posture (credential / auth mechanism) for that specific boundary-crossing REST call, leaving an undeclared access level on a live Gerrit admin API path.
findings.location: AC: 'Pre-cutover access baseline captured BEFORE the label is pushed'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the AC: 'The snapshot is fetched via HTTPS using the same admin credential as `setup-project.sh` (e.g., `curl -u admin:<HTTP-password-from-SSM> https://<host>/a/projects/rebar/access`); the admin HTTP password must not appear in the committed snapshot or the commit message.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC bullet 6's header-comment specification to explicitly require a note distinguishing the project-authored `Autosubmit` label from Gerrit's first-party `autosubmit` submit-plugin, and why the plugin is NOT used (to avoid auto-submit on gate satisfaction, which is the wrong semantic).
findings.finding: The name-collision warning between the project-authored `Autosubmit` label and Gerrit's first-party `autosubmit` submit-plugin is documented only in the plan's Why prose, not in any specified persisted artifact. The AC for the header comment (AC bullet 6) covers only the non-gating/re-verify security invariant; the naming distinction is not listed as required comment content.
findings.location: AC bullet 6 (security-invariants artifact) and Why section ('Name collision' callout)
findings.suggested_fix: Amend AC bullet 6 to read: '...the header comment MUST also record that this is a project-authored label distinct from Gerrit's first-party lowercase autosubmit submit-plugin, and that the plugin MUST NOT be installed alongside this label.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a rollback/abort procedure to the cutover AC: if the post-cutover diff contains anything beyond `label-Autosubmit` grants, specify the exact recovery step (e.g., `git push gerrit HEAD~1:refs/meta/config` or revert-and-re-push) before the change is considered complete.
findings.finding: The cutover AC requires the operator to verify the post-cutover access diff shows ONLY the added label-Autosubmit grants, but the plan provides no rollback procedure or explicit abort instruction if unexpected ACL drift is detected. An operator who finds extra drift has no specified recovery path.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'config-check passes; live cutover applied … a post-cutover GET … diffs cleanly against … showing ONLY the added label-Autosubmit vote grants (no other ACL drift)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a note under the cutover AC step: 'If the diff shows any change other than the two label-Autosubmit grant lines, immediately revert via `git push gerrit <pre-cutover-sha>:refs/meta/config` and investigate before re-attempting.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Document a RETIRE path in the Scope section: specify that removing the label requires (1) clearing all outstanding Autosubmit votes via the API, (2) removing the label block and ACL lines from project.config, and (3) pushing to refs/meta/config — or explicitly defer this to a named follow-on story.
findings.finding: The plan defines no RETIRE path for the `Autosubmit` label. If the auto-lander feature is discontinued, there is no specified procedure for removing the label definition, revoking the vote ACL, or cleaning up any open +1 votes that may exist on changes at that time.
findings.location: Scope section; Acceptance Criteria (no AC addresses label removal or feature retirement)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add at minimum a stub ADR (or a clearly-marked entry in CLAUDE.md) in S1 itself that records the Autosubmit-label-as-opt-in-lease architectural decision, non-gating rationale, and bot re-verify contract — even one paragraph — so the decision is anchored in the repo from the moment the config ships, with S6's ADR-0042 noted as the full consolidation.
findings.finding: S1 introduces the architectural decision (Autosubmit label as opt-in lease / non-gating mechanism for the auto-lander) but delivers no ADR or CLAUDE.md update itself, deferring entirely to S6's ADR-0042 with no fallback if S6 is delayed or dropped. The novel pattern is unanchored in any persisted doc until S6 ships.
findings.location: Scope section: 'The full ADR-0042 is authored downstream by S6 (broken-checkered-tiger) — S1 does NOT create or amend ADR-0042'
findings.suggested_fix: Create `docs/adr/ADR-0042-stub.md` (or add a section to CLAUDE.md) in S1's change set that records: (1) the Autosubmit label is the project's own non-gating opt-in lease, NOT Gerrit's first-party autosubmit plugin; (2) the bot requires `is:submittable` and re-verifies at submit; (3) ADR-0042 (S6) will be the canonical consolidation. This stub can be superseded/expanded by S6 without any conflict.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a prior-art note (in the Why or Scope section) acknowledging the AOSP/Chromium community `Autosubmit`/`Auto-Submit` project-label convention and explicitly state whether S1's design mirrors it (borrowing its -1..+1/NoBlock/bot-re-verify contract) or intentionally diverges from it.
findings.finding: The plan acknowledges the name collision with Gerrit's first-party `autosubmit` submit-plugin but does not acknowledge or contrast against the well-established AOSP/Chromium/Android community prior art of a project-authored `Autosubmit` (or `Auto-Submit`) label used for exactly the same 'opt-in queue for landing' semantic — same label name, -1..+1 range, NoBlock function, and 'bot re-verifies before submit' contract. The plan may be independently reinventing (or intentionally mirroring) a widely-adopted convention without citing it, leaving the reader unable to determine whether the design is intentionally aligned with or deliberately diverging from that established pattern.
findings.location: Why / What sections — 'Name collision' note and label design rationale
findings.suggested_fix: In the Why section, after the 'Name collision' note, add: 'Prior art: the AOSP/Chromium commit-queue ecosystem uses a project-authored `Autosubmit` (or `Auto-Submit`) label with the identical -1..+1/NoBlock/bot-re-verifies-before-submit contract. S1 intentionally mirrors that convention (lending established credibility to the security model) while adapting the ACL to this repo's Contributor/Administrator split.'

findings.finding: The plan's AC-6 directs the implementer to record the security-invariants entry inside 'the ADR-0042 Security invariants section', treating ADR-0042 as an already-existing document to amend. No such document exists anywhere in the repo (`docs/adr/` goes 0040 → 0041 → 0043 → 0044, with no 0042). The eval spec even explicitly notes 'no ticket it depends_on creates ADR-0042 — it is authored by a DOWNSTREAM story.' An AC that amends a non-existent document is an unbacked premise: either this story must CREATE ADR-0042 (which is undescribed work) or the AC must name the correct document where the invariant will be recorded.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'the ADR-0042 Security invariants section records ...' with 'Create `docs/adr/0042-autosubmit-label-security-invariants.md` and record the non-gating label + bot re-verifies invariant there, using the standard ADR template (Status, Context, Decision, Consequences). Add `grep` evidence as appendix.' This makes the deliverable explicit.

findings.finding: The plan's prose grants the `label-Autosubmit` ACL to 'Contributors (requesters) + Administrators' but the only canonical ACL line specified is `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors`. Every label in the existing `project.config` that has a vote grant also has a matching Administrators line (e.g., `label-LLM-Review = -1..+1 group Service Users` + `label-LLM-Review = -1..+1 group Administrators` at lines 46-47). Without an explicit `label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators` line, administrators cannot manually set or clear the Autosubmit label—breaking the operational fallback (e.g., manually revoking a stale Autosubmit+1 if the bot fails to clear it).
findings.location: ## What — ACL line: `[access "refs/heads/*"] label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors`; AC#2
findings.suggested_fix:
In the `[access "refs/heads/*"]` block, specify two lines:
```
label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Contributors
label-Autosubmit = -1..+1 group Administrators
```
Update AC#2 to list both lines explicitly.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
